title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Indigenous mezcal origin
description: Oaxaca — Zapotec + Mixtec distillation of agave predates Spanish arrival. Traditional clay/copper pot stills still used.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Indigenous mezcal origin
description: Valley of Mexico — Nahua pulque (fermented aguamiel) predates distilled mezcal by centuries. Sacred drink of Aztec priests.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Tequila denominación (Jalisco)
description: Tequila Jalisco — town gives name to blue agave spirit. 1974 Mexico codifies Tequila DO (Denominación de Origen).

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Tequila denominación (Jalisco)
description: Guadalajara — Jalisco state capital, anchor of the Tequila industry.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Mezcal DO (Oaxaca + 8 other states)
description: Oaxaca — mezcal DO. Protected by Mexican law 1994; now covers 9 states.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Prohibition → modern export boom
description: Tijuana border — US Prohibition (1920–1933) makes Mexico the booze destination; Tequila export begins.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Prohibition → modern export boom
description: Los Angeles — margarita cocktail born in border-town bars 1940s.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Prohibition → modern export boom
description: New York — 2010s premium-agave boom. Tequila becomes $10B+ global category.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: Nahuatl roots
description: Zapotec + Mixtec — each group maintains distinct agave names + fermentation traditions.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: Nahuatl roots
description: Spanish — preserves Nahuatl words 'tequila' + 'mezcal' in global trade.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Tequila Jalisco — ~95% of tequila production. Blue agave monoculture has created ecological pressure.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Oaxaca — mezcal heartland; 50+ agave species used vs. one for tequila. Mostly small-batch, high-ceremony.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: NYC / LA — US is world's largest tequila market (75% of exports).

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Miami — celebrity tequila brands (Casamigos, 818, Teremana) built on US marketing, Jalisco supply.
